PDF atom contains only the first clause of the full rule; MD candidate md_ch02_a077 includes both this clause and the follow-on 'even if null' condition — MD is more complete than the PDF atom, but PDF atom is only a subset sentence. Content present.

PDF atom contains two sub-clauses: (1) must include permissible variable even if null, and (2) refer to Define-XML for no-data management. MD candidate md_ch02_a077 covers clause 1 only; md_ch02_a068 covers clause 2. No single candidate covers both sub-clauses together.

PDF atom 'To create a custom domain,' is a sentence fragment (introduction to the list). KB candidate md_ch02_a081 covers the introductory framing equivalently. Content essentially captured but only as part of a larger sentence in KB.

PDF atom contains core rule plus examples (microbiology, tumor measurements, etc.). MD candidate md_ch02_a082 contains the full rule including examples — MD is actually more complete. PDF atom is a partial sentence of the full rule; KB covers it. Verdict EQUIVALENT is appropriate since MD covers all PDF content.

PDF atom covers grouping by topic/nature with variable list and mentions 'electrocardiogram' example and 'examples of different' (truncated). MD candidate md_ch02_a083 captures core rule (group by topic, variables list) but omits the electrocardiogram example and the continuation text.

PDF atom contains the full rule plus two concrete examples (blood pressure in hypertension study must go to VS; liver function tests must go to LB). MD candidate md_ch02_a100 captures the rule ('Do not create efficacy domains') but omits the specific examples.

PDF atom is a long rule explaining domain pairs (MB/MS, PC/PP), the role of DOMAIN as identifier, RELREC usage, and why DOMAIN is needed. MD candidate md_ch02_a102 captures only the DOMAIN-as-identifier/RELREC summary. The explanation of RELREC mechanism and the constraint reasoning is omitted.

PDF atom includes the full CDISC wiki URL and states proposed domains may be used as custom domains in a submission. MD candidate md_ch02_a085 captures the core instruction (check CDISC wiki for proposed domains) but omits the URL and the explicit statement that proposed domains may be used as custom domains.

PDF atom describes the prototype-search process plus the GOC selection logic and references a figure. MD candidate md_ch02_a086 captures only the top-level instruction (look for prototype, follow steps). The GOC selection rationale and figure reference are absent.

PDF atom contains the full domain-code selection rule including: not in CT codelist, URLs for CT and CDISC submission form, 2-character consistency rule, and prohibited codes (AD, AX, AP, SQ, SA). MD candidate md_ch02_a091 captures only the essential constraints (not in CT codelist; AD, AX, AP, SQ, SA prohibited) — omits the URLs and the CT submission request process.

PDF atom covers: (1) place non-standard variables in SUPP-- dataset, and (2) describes mechanism for representing additional non-standard qualifier variables and defining relationships via Section 8.4. MD candidate md_ch02_a097 covers only point 1 (place non-standard variables in SUPP--). The cross-reference to Section 8.4 and the relationship mechanism context are absent.

PDF atom is a figure caption 'Figure. Creating a New Domain'. No standalone figure-caption atom exists in KB; top candidate md_ch02_a079 is the section heading '## 2.6 Creating a New Domain', not a figure caption.

PDF atom introduces §2.7 scope: identifies two categories of restricted variables (1) should not be used in clinical tabulations, (2) not yet evaluated for human clinical trials. No candidate captures this introductory scope sentence; top candidates are off-topic (score 0.43, different sections).

PDF atom is the introductory sentence listing SEND-only variables that must NEVER be used in human clinical trials. No candidate in KB covers this specific introductory sentence; top candidates are unrelated (score 0.44, §2.1 context).
PDF atom is for RPPLDY alone. KB candidate md_ch02_a117 groups RPPLDY, RPPLSTDY, and RPPLENDY in a single row. Content is present but merged with two other variables.
PDF atom is for RPPLSTDY alone. KB candidate md_ch02_a117 groups RPPLDY, RPPLSTDY, and RPPLENDY in a single row. Content is present but merged with two other variables.
PDF atom is for RPPLENDY alone. KB candidate md_ch02_a117 groups RPPLDY, RPPLSTDY, and RPPLENDY in a single row. Content is present but merged with two other variables.
PDF atom is for --NOMDY alone. KB candidate md_ch02_a118 groups --NOMDY and --NOMLBL in a single row. Content is present but merged with another variable.
PDF atom is for --NOMLBL alone. KB candidate md_ch02_a118 groups --NOMDY and --NOMLBL in a single row. Content is present but merged with another variable.
PDF atom is for --RPDY alone. KB candidate md_ch02_a119 groups --RPDY, --RPSTDY, and --RPENDY in a single row. Content is present but merged with two other variables.
PDF atom is for --RPSTDY alone. KB candidate md_ch02_a119 groups --RPDY, --RPSTDY, and --RPENDY in a single row. Content is present but merged with two other variables.
PDF atom is for --RPENDY alone. KB candidate md_ch02_a119 groups --RPDY, --RPSTDY, and --RPENDY in a single row. Content is present but merged with two other variables.

PDF atom is the introductory sentence for SEND variables prohibited in DM domain for human clinical trials. No candidate covers this framing sentence; all candidates score <0.40 and are from unrelated sections.

PDF atom is SPECIES (Demographics) as a standalone entry. KB candidate md_ch02_a123 groups SPECIES, STRAIN, SBSTRAIN, RPATHCD together in one list item without the '(Demographics)' qualifier. Content present but merged and category label omitted.
PDF atom is STRAIN (Demographics) as a standalone entry. KB candidate md_ch02_a123 groups SPECIES, STRAIN, SBSTRAIN, RPATHCD together. Content present but merged and category label omitted.
PDF atom is SBSTRAIN (Demographics) as a standalone entry. KB candidate md_ch02_a123 groups SPECIES, STRAIN, SBSTRAIN, RPATHCD together. Content present but merged and category label omitted.
PDF atom is RPATHCD (Demographics) as a standalone entry. KB candidate md_ch02_a123 groups SPECIES, STRAIN, SBSTRAIN, RPATHCD together. Content present but merged and category label omitted.

PDF atom is SETCD (Demographics) as standalone entry. KB candidate md_ch02_a128 correctly groups it with the note about requiring Trial Sets domain. Content is present and expanded in KB, but KB merges the standalone variable entry with its note into one atom.

PDF atom is the introductory sentence for the SEND identifier variable allowed in human clinical trials when appropriate (POOLID section). No candidate covers this framing sentence; all candidates score <0.44 and are from unrelated sections.
PDF atom is POOLID as a standalone entry. KB candidate md_ch02_a130 merges POOLID with the note about Pool Definition dataset. Content present but merged with its note.

PDF atom is a full sentence stating dataset definition metadata should include filenames/descriptions/locations/structures/class/purpose/keys per Section 3.2.1. MD candidate md_ch03_a019 is a list item capturing the fields. MD candidate md_ch03_a018 is the lead-in sentence. Together they cover the PDF atom but no single candidate fully captures both the instruction and the enumeration.

PDF atom key field: CODTC. KB atom key field: COOTC. One character difference (D vs O) in a controlled variable name. Could be a transcription error in either source. Per instructions, when in doubt between PARTIAL and ERROR, use PARTIAL.
